description: Distribution-theoretic facts from Saporito Appendix B (Multivariate Gaussian and Exponential)

theorems.name: Marginal of Multivariate Normal is Normal
theorems.description: Theorem B.1.2(1): If X = DW + μ is multivariate normal with W iid N(0,1), then each X_i ~ N(μ_i, Σ_{ii}) where Σ = DD^T

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Matrix EuclideanSpace

/-- Theorem B.1.2(1) (Marginal of multivariate Gaussian).
    For a covariance matrix `S` that is positive semidefinite, the `i`-th
    coordinate marginal of `multivariateGaussian μ S` is the one-dimensional
    Gaussian `gaussianReal (μ i) (S i i).toNNReal`.

    Direct wrapper of Mathlib's
    `ProbabilityTheory.measurePreserving_eval_multivariateGaussian`. -/
theorem multivariateGaussian_marginal_is_gaussian
    {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι]
    (μ : EuclideanSpace ℝ ι) {S : Matrix ι ι ℝ} (hS : S.PosSemidef) (i : ι) :
    Measure.map (fun x : EuclideanSpace ℝ ι => x i) (multivariateGaussian μ S)
      = gaussianReal (μ i) (S i i).toNNReal :=
  (measurePreserving_eval_multivariateGaussian (μ := μ) hS (i := i)).map_eq

metadata.formalization_scope: Lean library wrapper of Mathlib master `Mathlib.Probability.Distributions.Gaussian.Multivariate.measurePreserving_eval_multivariateGaussian`. The textbook formulation X = D·W + μ matches `multivariateGaussian μ S` with S = D·Dᵀ; the marginal-is-Gaussian conclusion is exactly the `map_eq` field of the measure-preserving statement. Requires Lean v4.30+ (multivariateGaussian landed at v4.29.0-rc6).

theorems.name: Affine Transformation of Multivariate Normal
theorems.description: Theorem B.1.2(3): If X is multivariate normal with mean μ and covariance Σ, and Y = CX + d, then Y is multivariate normal with mean Cμ + d and covariance CΣC^T

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

-- 1D affine instances of Theorem B.1.2(3) for real Gaussians (Mathlib v4.30):
--   If X has law N(μ, v) under P and Y = c·X, then Y has law N(c·μ, c²·v).
--   If X has law N(μ, v) under P and Y = X + y, then Y has law N(μ + y, v).
-- Mathlib v4.30 expresses these via `HasLaw X (gaussianReal μ v) P`; the
-- prior `Measure.map` form is no longer the surface API.
example {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω}
    {μ : ℝ} {v : NNReal} {X : Ω → ℝ}
    (hX : HasLaw X (gaussianReal μ v) P) (c : ℝ) :
    HasLaw (fun ω ↦ c * X ω)
      (gaussianReal (c * μ) (.mk (c ^ 2) (sq_nonneg _) * v)) P :=
  gaussianReal_const_mul hX c

example {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω}
    {μ : ℝ} {v : NNReal} {X : Ω → ℝ}
    (hX : HasLaw X (gaussianReal μ v) P) (y : ℝ) :
    HasLaw (fun ω ↦ X ω + y) (gaussianReal (μ + y) v) P :=
  gaussianReal_add_const hX y

metadata.formalization_scope: Direct Lean wrapper around an existing library theorem closely matching the benchmark statement.

theorems.name: Conditional Distribution of Bivariate Gaussian
theorems.description: Theorem B.1.3(2): For (X,Y) jointly Gaussian with Σ_{YY} > 0, E[X|Y] = μ_X + Σ_{XY} Σ_{YY}^{-1}(Y - μ_Y)

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.BivariateGaussian

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Theorem B.1.3 (2): for a bivariate Gaussian pair (X, Y) with positive
    marginal variances and correlation ρ ∈ (−1, 1), the conditional expectation
    of X given σ(Y) is μ_X + (ρ σ_X / σ_Y)(Y − μ_Y) almost surely.
    Re-export of `MathFin.BivariateGaussianHyp.conditional_expectation_formula`
    (real derivation in `MathFin/Foundations/BivariateGaussian.lean`: orthogonal
    regression, joint-Gaussian transfer via a linear CLM, Cov = 0 ⇒
    `HasGaussianLaw.indepFun_of_covariance_eq_zero`, then condExp linearity). -/
theorem bivariate_gaussian_conditional_expectation
    {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}
    {P : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {X Y : Ω → ℝ} {μ_X μ_Y σ_X σ_Y ρ : ℝ}
    (h : MathFin.BivariateGaussianHyp P X Y μ_X μ_Y σ_X σ_Y ρ) :
    (P[X | MeasurableSpace.comap Y inferInstance])
      =ᵐ[P] fun ω => μ_X + (ρ * σ_X / σ_Y) * (Y ω - μ_Y) :=
  h.conditional_expectation_formula

metadata.formalization_scope: Real Lean derivation of Theorem B.1.3(2). The hypotheses (joint Gaussianity of (X,Y), marginal means, Var Y, Cov(X,Y)) are bundled in `BivariateGaussianHyp`; the conditional-expectation conclusion is *derived* (not a structural field) via orthogonal regression β = ρσ_X/σ_Y, linear-CLM transfer of joint Gaussianity to (X − βY, Y), Cov(X − βY, Y) = 0 ⇒ independence via `HasGaussianLaw.indepFun_of_covariance_eq_zero`, `condExp_indep_eq` for the orthogonal residual, `condExp_of_stronglyMeasurable` for the σ(Y)-measurable estimator, and `condExp_add`/`condExp_sub` linearity. Proof lives in `MathFin/Foundations/BivariateGaussian.lean` (~240 lines).
metadata.lean_note: Promoted reduced_core → full on 2026-05-13: structure field `conditional_mean` removed; the conclusion is now derived inside MathFin.BivariateGaussian.

theorems.name: Memoryless Property of Exponential
theorems.description: Appendix B.2: τ ~ Exp(λ) ⇔ τ is memoryless: P(τ > t + s | τ > s) = P(τ > t) for all s, t ≥ 0

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open ProbabilityTheory Real MeasureTheory

-- Distribution-level memoryless identity for Exp(r), expressed via the
-- abstract CDF of `expMeasure r` (Mathlib v4.30): for s, t ≥ 0,
--   (1 - F(s+t)) / (1 - F(s)) = 1 - F(t),
-- where F = cdf (expMeasure r). Equivalently P(τ > s+t | τ > s) = P(τ > t).
example {r s t : ℝ} (hr : 0 < r) (hs : 0 ≤ s) (ht : 0 ≤ t) :
    (1 - cdf (expMeasure r) (s + t)) / (1 - cdf (expMeasure r) s)
      = 1 - cdf (expMeasure r) t := by
  rw [cdf_expMeasure_eq hr (s + t), cdf_expMeasure_eq hr s, cdf_expMeasure_eq hr t]
  simp [hs, ht, add_nonneg hs ht]
  field_simp [Real.exp_ne_zero]
  rw [← Real.exp_add]
  congr 1
  ring

metadata.formalization_scope: Faithful formal statement/proof for the benchmark theorem as encoded, using Mathlib exponential CDF infrastructure.

theorems.name: Minimum of Independent Exponentials
theorems.description: Appendix B.2: If τ_1, ..., τ_n independent with τ_i ~ Exp(λ_i), then min(τ_1,...,τ_n) ~ Exp(λ_1+...+λ_n)

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.ExpMin

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Appendix B.2: minimum of jointly independent exponential random variables
    has Exp(∑ rates) at the survival-function level. Re-export of
    `MathFin.minimum_survival` (real derivation in
    `MathFin/Foundations/ExpMin.lean`, derived from `iIndepFun.meas_iInter`
    and the `expMeasure` CDF formula). -/
theorem minimum_of_independent_exponentials_survival
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω}
    {n : ℕ} {rates : Fin n → ℝ} {τ : Fin n → Ω → ℝ}
    (hrates_pos : ∀ i, 0 < rates i)
    (hexp_law : ∀ i, Measure.map (τ i) μ = expMeasure (rates i))
    (hindep : iIndepFun τ μ)
    (hmeas : ∀ i, Measurable (τ i))
    (hn : 0 < n) {t : ℝ} (ht : 0 ≤ t) :
    μ {ω | t < Finset.univ.inf' ⟨⟨0, hn⟩, Finset.mem_univ _⟩
      (fun i : Fin n => τ i ω)} =
        ENNReal.ofReal (Real.exp (-((∑ i, rates i) * t))) :=
  MathFin.minimum_survival hrates_pos hexp_law hindep hmeas hn ht

metadata.formalization_scope: Survival-function identity for the minimum of jointly independent exponentials, derived from joint independence (iIndepFun.meas_iInter) and individual exponential laws (exp_law + expMeasure CDF formula). At the survival-function (CDF) level, matching the precedent of dist-exp-memoryless. The structure no longer axiomatizes the minimum's distribution as a field; the conclusion is derived.
